output.summary: Sodium-ion batteries serve as a cost-effective, abundant alternative to lithium-ion technology, though they currently offer lower energy density. Lithium-ion batteries remain the industry standard for high-performance applications, while solid-state batteries represent the emerging frontier with the highest potential energy density. In a comparative ranking, solid-state batteries lead the field, followed by lithium-ion, with sodium-ion batteries trailing in energy capacity. Consequently, solid-state technology is identified as the highest-energy-density option among the three.

Energy density ranking: Solid-state > Lithium-ion > Sodium-ion.
Solid-state is the highest-energy-density technology among the three.

output.question: Provide a detailed overview of sodium-ion batteries, including a comparison of energy density against Lithium-Ion and Solid-State batteries.

sources.title: Tech Evolution of Energy Storage Batteries: Lithium-ion vs. Sodium-ion vs. Solid-state

output.findings:
Sodium-ion batteries are an emerging energy storage technology that leverages the high abundance and low cost of sodium compared to lithium. They are characterized by improved thermal stability and safety, making them particularly attractive for stationary grid-scale storage and cost-sensitive, low-speed electric mobility applications. While currently in the early stages of commercialization, they are seen as a critical alternative to mitigate supply chain risks associated with lithium.

In terms of performance metrics, sodium-ion batteries generally possess a lower energy density than current lithium-ion technology. Lithium-ion batteries remain the market standard due to their mature manufacturing, high energy density, and long cycle life. Solid-state batteries, which utilize a solid electrolyte instead of a liquid one, represent the frontier of battery research. They are projected to offer the highest energy densities and superior safety profiles, though they currently face significant challenges regarding manufacturing complexity and high costs, limiting them primarily to high-end or specialized applications.

| Battery Technology | Relative Energy Density | Primary Application Focus |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Solid-State | Highest | Next-gen EVs, Aerospace |
| Lithium-Ion | High | Current EVs, Residential Storage |
| Sodium-Ion | Moderate (Lower) | Grid Storage, Low-speed Mobility |

Ranking of energy density (highest to lowest): Solid-State > Lithium-Ion > Sodium-Ion. The technology with the highest energy density is Solid-State.
